2603.20133.title: Reasoning Gets Harder for LLMs Inside A Dialogue
2603.20133.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) achieve strong performance on many reasoning benchmarks, yet these evaluations typically focus on isolated tasks that differ from real-world usage in task-oriented dialogue (TOD). In this setting, LLMs must perform reasoning inherently while generating text and adhering to instructions on role, format, and style. This mismatch raises concerns about whether benchmark performance accurately reflects models' reasoning robustness in TOD setting. We investigate how framing reasoning tasks within TOD affects LLM performance by introducing BOULDER, a new dynamic benchmark covering eight travel-related tasks that require arithmetic, spatial, and temporal reasoning with both commonsense and formal aspects. Each problem is presented in both isolated and dialogue-based variants, enabling controlled comparison while mitigating data contamination. Experiments on eight LLMs reveal a substantial and consistent performance gap between isolated and dialogue settings. Through ablations and qualitative analysis, we show that this gap is largely driven by the multi-turn nature of dialogue, with additional effects from role conditioning and tool-use requirements. Our results highlight the need to evaluate LLM reasoning in realistic interactive scenarios.
2603.20133.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在許多推理基準上表現出色，然而這些評估通常專注於與實際任務導向對話（TOD）使用情境不同的孤立任務。<lb/>在這種情境下，LLMs 必須在生成文本的同時，自然地進行推理，並遵循角色、格式和風格的指示。<lb/>這種不匹配引發了人們對基準性能是否準確反映模型在 TOD 環境中推理穩健性的擔憂。<lb/>我們通過引入 BOULDER 來研究在 TOD 中框定推理任務如何影響 LLM 的性能，這是一個新的動態基準，涵蓋八個與旅行相關的任務，這些任務需要進行算術、空間和時間推理，並結合常識和正式方面。<lb/>每個問題都以孤立和基於對話的變體呈現，這使得在控制比較的同時減少數據污染。<lb/>對八個 LLM 的實驗顯示，孤立和對話環境之間存在顯著且一致的性能差距。<lb/>通過消融實驗和定性分析，我們顯示這一差距主要是由於對話的多輪性質所驅動，並且角色條件和工具使用要求也有額外影響。<lb/>我們的結果強調了在現實互動場景中評估 LLM 推理的必要性。
2603.20133.author: Ivan Kartáč et.al.
2603.20133.authors: Ivan Kartáč, Mateusz Lango, Ondřej Dušek
